problem_title: Suspicious activity reviews slow compliance workflows
problem_description: VASP compliance teams struggle to connect fragmented alert triage with SAR and STR drafting processes. This gap creates manual delays, inconsistent case evidence, and weak audit readiness across suspicious transaction reporting workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates alert triage, case evidence, reviewer decisions and SAR drafting into a single workflow, enabling VASP compliance teams to streamline suspicious activity reporting with stronger audit readiness.
workflow_title: From alerts to SAR reporting workflow
workflow_description: SFTY structures alerts into cases routed for review with documented decisions and evidence preserved to prepare SAR-style reports for compliance teams.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another provider. The compliance control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence to streamline suspicious activity review and SAR/STR reporting for VASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow

authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams with a connected platform that connects KYC, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening outputs into structured workflows, supporting case management and SAR-style reporting.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support suspicious activity reviews?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures suspicious activity reviews by aggregating alerts, provider outputs and regulatory triggers into cases, supporting escalation, decision logging and preparation of SAR draft reports within one workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve SAR audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit evidence by maintaining decision logs, linked provider data, case history and review trails, supporting SAR investigations are well documented and review-ready for internal and external audits.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, connecting their outputs without replacement while providing role-based access, audit logs and configurable workflows.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYT providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC, KYT or screening providers but connects and structures their outputs into compliant workflows and evidence packages for VASPs to manage SAR reporting.
final_title: Structure VASP SAR workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects suspicious activity alerts, case management and SAR drafting into one audit-ready compliance workflow.
